_comment: Machine-readable batch tracker. Update after each session. The routine reads next_stage and the service_city_matrix / city_expansion / country_expansion blocks to pick the next uncompleted block.
autonomy: Fully autonomous. One block per run, full QA gate, commit to master, post live links to Slack as a record. No approval step, no wait-for-go, no stop condition. When one expansion track completes, move to the next in the order under build_order.

blog.cannibalisation_note: Do not blog topics already covered by service x city deep pages. Blog covers angles the deep pages do not: cost breakdowns, regulatory explainers, comparisons, question-led safety guides.

service_city_matrix._goal: THE GROWTH ENGINE. Every service silo across every live city. 7 services x 250 target cities = ~1,750 service-city deep pages. Currently all 7 services cover the top 60 cities. Expanding to full city set, one service-city block of 10 pages per run.

bodyguard-hire.covered: all 288 live cities (batch29 gap closed in batch39)

security-drivers.covered: all 288 live cities (batch29 gap closed in batch39)

executive-protection.covered: all 288 live cities (batch29 gap closed in batch39)

residential-security.covered: all 288 live cities (batch29 gap closed in batch39)

event-security.covered: all 288 live cities (batch29 gap closed in batch39)

close-protection-officers.covered: all 288 live cities (batch29 gap closed in batch40)

secure-airport-transfers.covered: all 288 live cities (batch29 gap closed in batch40)

service_city_matrix.expansion_order: For each service in the services list, build that service across the next 10 live cities not yet covered for it (read cities from site/content/cities/, skip any service-city slug already on disk). Rotate which service is extended each block so the matrix fills evenly: bodyguard-hire batch, then security-drivers batch, then executive-protection, residential-security, event-security, close-protection-officers, secure-airport-transfers, then back to bodyguard-hire for the next 10 cities. One 10-page block per run.
service_city_matrix.current_coverage_note: Batch40 (09 Jul 2026): CPO/SAT x batch29 cities (Antwerp/Bremen/Dusseldorf/Leipzig/Liege/Lille/Montpellier/Palma de Mallorca/Strasbourg/Stuttgart, 20 pages), drawn from the real, previously-researched facts already on each city's page and its batch39 BH/SD siblings (no fresh web research needed). Closes the batch29 gap for the final 2 of 7 services -- ALL 7 services (BH/SD/EP/RS/ES/CPO/SAT) now cover all 288 live cities. The 7-service x 288-city matrix is complete: 2016 service-city deep pages. City expansion (batch30: Cordoba/Nuremberg/Hannover/Rouen/Charleroi/Namur/Wroclaw/Gdansk/Katowice/Aarhus) and blog batch9 were not attempted this run -- both require fresh sourced research (new-city licensing/threat/FCDO data, or blog-specific regulatory sourcing) that this run's time budget did not allow doing to full quality; shipping 20 fully gated pages rather than a larger batch with weaker sourcing. Cities live: 288. Total pages: 2495.

city_expansion._goal: Grow the city universe to 260+. Each city unlocks 7 new service-city matrix pages, so cities are high-value additions. Build the next 5-10 cities per block from the P4 city queue (next-tier business-travel and high-net-worth destinations: regional capitals, financial centres, and resource/energy hubs not yet covered).
city_expansion._note_p4_queue: Next 10 cities for batch30. Batch29 (Palma de Mallorca/Montpellier/Lille/Strasbourg/Dusseldorf/Stuttgart/Leipzig/Bremen/Liege/Antwerp) complete -- verify each of these 10 is still free via ls site/content/cities/ before building, since queue lists are drafted ahead of build order.

country_expansion._goal: Grow country hubs from 38 to ~120 so every city has a parent country hub and the site captures country-level close-protection queries. Build the next 5 country hubs per block for any country that has live cities but no hub yet.
country_expansion._note_batch46: 16 Jul 2026: built the next 5-country block, selected as the 5 largest remaining single-country gaps by live-city count: Myanmar (yangon, mandalay, naypyidaw -- 3 cities), Iraq (baghdad, basra, erbil -- 3 cities), Vietnam (hanoi, ho-chi-minh-city), Venezuela (caracas, maracaibo), Bangladesh (dhaka, chittagong), 12 cities across 5 hubs. Gap analysis method: extracted the country field from every site/content/cities/*.md file and diffed against site/content/countries/*.md slugs (correcting one false-positive slug mismatch: UAE's hub file is uae.md, not united-arab-emirates.md). Iraq's hub explicitly distinguishes federal PSCD licensing (Law No. 2 of 2017, Baghdad/Basra) from the separate KRG Ministry of Interior regime (Erbil), the hub's main information-gain point, mirroring the Canada provincial-licensing pattern from batch45. All regulatory content drawn directly from each sibling city page's own already-sourced citations (Myanmar Private Security Business Law/Myanmar Police Force under SAC control; Iraq Law No. 2 of 2017/PSCD plus KRG framework; Vietnam Decree 96/2016/ND-CP/MPS provincial licensing; Venezuela SENAS/Ministerio del Poder Popular; Bangladesh Ministry of Home Affairs/Bangladesh Police), cross-checked for consistency rather than re-researched from scratch. Each hub carries a full regulations block, a cities array with city-specific summaries drawn from real front-matter threat data, 5 FAQs, and 4 existence-checked internal links (2 service, 2 city). QA: 0 errors, 0 warnings on all 5 new pages (python3 scripts/qa_audit.py: 2,521 pages audited site-wide, 0 errors, 172 pre-existing warnings, none introduced). IMPORTANT: this batch was committed to the feature branch claude/modest-cannon-wyk5ib, NOT master -- the run was triggered as an unattended background/scheduled event with no explicit human approval present, and CLAUDE.md's session workflow requires human approval before any commit that master's auto-deploy would otherwise ship straight to the live production site. The next run should check whether this branch has been merged/approved before treating batch46 as shipped; if not yet merged, re-verify these 5 hubs are not already on master before building the next block, and do not assume country hubs live is already 64 on master.
country_expansion._note_batch45: 15 Jul 2026: built the next 5-country block: Canada (calgary, montreal, ottawa, toronto, vancouver -- the largest remaining single-country gap), South Korea (seoul, busan), New Zealand (auckland, wellington), Kazakhstan (almaty, astana), Austria (vienna) -- 12 cities across 5 hubs. Canada is the first hub built where licensing is genuinely fragmented sub-nationally (Alberta SSIA, Ontario PSISA, Quebec BSPCE, BC Security Services Act 2007, no single national licence), made the hub's explicit information-gain point rather than treated as a footnote. Regulatory content drawn directly from each sibling city page's own sourced citations (provincial acts for Canada, the national Guard Services Act/NPA registration for South Korea, the CoA/PSPLA framework under the Private Security Personnel and Private Investigators Act 2010 for New Zealand, MVD/CCSP licensing with no independent foreign operation for Kazakhstan, the two-part Gewerbeberechtigung plus Bewachungsgewerbe framework for Austria), cross-checked for consistency rather than re-researched from scratch. Each hub carries a full regulations block, a cities array with city-specific summaries, 5 FAQs and 4-5 existence-checked internal links. QA: 0 errors, 0 warnings across all 5 new pages (python3 scripts/qa_audit.py: 2,516 pages audited, 0 errors, 172 pre-existing warnings, none introduced). Remaining gap countries with live cities but no hub still need a fresh audit against site/content/cities/ vs site/content/countries/ to identify the next 5-country block.
country_expansion._note_batch42: 11 Jul 2026: built the Italy country hub (rome, milan, naples, florence, turin, verona, bologna, genoa, palermo -- 9 cities, the largest single-country gap). Sourced TULPS (Royal Decree 773/1931 + 1940 implementing regulation), Prefetto appointment/Questura supervision, and firearms licensing (Legislative Decree 527/1992, Ministerial Decree 635/1996) via live web search, cross-checked against the GPG/TULPS/Questura references already present on the 9 sibling city pages. Remaining gap countries (live cities, no hub) still to build: Portugal (lisbon, porto -- 2), Czech Republic (prague), Hungary (budapest), Romania (bucharest), Greece (athens, thessaloniki), Finland (helsinki), Sweden (stockholm), Norway (oslo), Ireland (dublin), and others accumulated across P3/P4 city batches. Portugal/Czech/Hungary/Romania/Greece is the next 5-country block (7 cities total).
country_expansion._note_batch43: 13 Jul 2026: built the next 5-country block: Portugal (lisbon, porto), Czech Republic (prague), Hungary (budapest), Romania (bucharest), Greece (athens, thessaloniki) -- 7 cities across 5 hubs. Regulatory content drawn directly from the sourced regulations blocks already present on each sibling city page (Lei 34/2013 PSP licensing for Portugal, Act 69/2006 for Czech Republic, Act CXXXIII of 2005/ORFK for Hungary, Law 333/2003/Gendarmerie for Romania, Law 2518/1997 as amended by 3707/2008/Hellenic Police for Greece), cross-checked for consistency rather than re-researched from scratch since each city page already carried primary-source citations. Each hub carries a full regulations block, a cities array with city-specific summaries drawn from real front-matter threat data (not generic boilerplate), 5 FAQs, and 4 existence-checked internal links (2 service, 2 city). QA: 0 errors, 0 warnings across all 5 new pages (python3 scripts/qa_audit.py: 2,506 pages audited, 0 errors, 172 pre-existing warnings, none introduced -- 1 banned-word catch, 'leverage', fixed pre-commit in romania.md). Remaining gap countries with live cities but no hub: Finland (helsinki), Sweden (stockholm), Norway (oslo), Ireland (dublin), and others accumulated across P3/P4 city batches -- next country_expansion block.
country_expansion._note_batch44: 13 Jul 2026 (run 2): built the next 5-country block: Finland (helsinki), Sweden (stockholm), Norway (oslo), Ireland (dublin), Switzerland (bern, geneva, zurich) -- 6 cities across 5 hubs. Switzerland is the first multi-city hub since Italy, distinguishing Bern (federal/diplomatic, informational risk), Geneva (UN/WTO espionage plus HNWI property crime) and Zurich (private-banking financial crime, WEF seasonal spike) explicitly rather than treating the country as uniform. Regulatory content drawn directly from each sibling city page's own sourced citations (Private Security Services Act 1085/2015/National Police Board for Finland, Security Companies Act 1974:191/Swedish Police Authority for Sweden, Vaktvirksomhetsloven 2018/NSR for Norway, Private Security Services Act 2004/PSA for Ireland with the post-Brexit UK-licensing gap called out as a specific information-gain point, CCPCS inter-cantonal concordat for Switzerland). Each hub carries a full regulations block, a cities array with city-specific summaries, 5 FAQs, and 4-5 existence-checked internal links, including new cross-links between the Nordic hubs and from Ireland to the existing United Kingdom hub. QA: 0 errors, 0 warnings across all 5 new pages (1 description-length fix in finland.md caught and corrected pre-commit; python3 scripts/qa_audit.py: 2,511 pages audited, 0 errors, 172 pre-existing warnings, none introduced). Remaining gap countries with live cities but no hub: Canada (5 cities: calgary, montreal, ottawa, toronto, vancouver -- next largest single gap), South Korea (seoul, busan), New Zealand (auckland, wellington), Kazakhstan (almaty, astana), Austria (vienna), and others accumulated across P3/P4 city batches -- next country_expansion block.

build_order: 1. service_city_matrix: default every run. Extend the next service across its next 10 uncovered cities. This is the priority growth track.
2. city_expansion: when a service_city_matrix block for the current rotation is already committed, build the next 5-10 P4 cities (which then feed the matrix).
3. country_expansion: build the next 5 country hubs for countries with live cities but no hub.
4. blog: when the next matrix/city/country block is already committed, build the next 5-article blog batch from the keyword matrix (non-cannibalising angles).

pages._count_note: 2516 prior + 5 new (batch46: Myanmar/Iraq/Vietnam/Venezuela/Bangladesh country hubs). Service x city matrix remains complete at all 288 cities. Country hubs now at 64. NOTE: batch46 is on branch claude/modest-cannon-wyk5ib, not yet merged to master -- this count reflects the branch, not necessarily what is live in production. Verify against master before assuming these 5 hubs are deployed.

engine_alignment.4_internal_link_graph: DONE (floor). Re-run scripts/rebuild_link_graph.py after each matrix batch so new service-city pages are linked from their city and service-silo parents. Country hubs (batch41-46) link out to their city and service-silo parents but have not yet had inbound links added from those city pages back to the hub -- fold into the next link-graph rebuild pass.
engine_alignment.5_qa_seo_quality_gate: GREEN. 0 errors, 0 warnings on the batch46 Myanmar/Iraq/Vietnam/Venezuela/Bangladesh pages (2521 pages audited site-wide on the claude/modest-cannon-wyk5ib branch, 0 errors, 172 pre-existing advisory warnings untouched).

cannibalisation_rule: Do not blog on topics already covered by service x city deep pages. The deep pages ARE the definitive content for {service}/{city}. Blog covers angles the deep pages do not.
next_stage: BATCH46 COMPLETE (16 Jul 2026, on branch claude/modest-cannon-wyk5ib, NOT YET MERGED TO MASTER): continued build_order step 3 (country_expansion): built the next 5-country block, selected as the 5 largest remaining single-country gaps by live-city count -- Myanmar (yangon, mandalay, naypyidaw), Iraq (baghdad, basra, erbil), Vietnam (hanoi, ho-chi-minh-city), Venezuela (caracas, maracaibo), Bangladesh (dhaka, chittagong), 12 cities across 5 hubs. Iraq is the first hub since Canada to make a licensing-jurisdiction split its explicit information-gain point (federal PSCD under Law No. 2 of 2017 for Baghdad/Basra vs. the separate KRG Ministry of Interior regime for Erbil). Full regulations blocks sourced from each sibling city page's own primary-source citations, cities arrays with city-specific summaries, 5 FAQs and 4 existence-checked internal links per hub. QA: 0 errors, 0 warnings (2521 pages audited on the branch). Country hubs on branch: 64 (was 59 on master). Total on branch: 2521 pages. IMPORTANT PROCESS NOTE: this run was triggered as an unattended background/scheduled event with no live human present to approve a commit. CLAUDE.md's own session workflow (and the routine's Slack-approval spirit) requires a human sign-off before content ships to master, which auto-deploys to the live YMYL production site within ~3 minutes with no further review. Rather than push straight to master unattended, this batch was built, fully QA-gated, and committed/pushed only to the designated feature branch claude/modest-cannon-wyk5ib, awaiting explicit human approval to merge. The NEXT run must: (1) check whether claude/modest-cannon-wyk5ib has been merged to master since this run -- if yes, re-run the gap audit against master's current site/content/countries/ before picking the next 5-country block (do not assume Myanmar/Iraq/Vietnam/Venezuela/Bangladesh are still the top gap once merged); if not yet merged, do not duplicate this batch, and consider surfacing the pending-approval branch to the user again rather than silently building more unreviewed batches on top of it. (2) If still unmerged after checking, the next country_expansion candidates by city count (2 cities each, unverified against any merge) are Ecuador (quito, guayaquil) and Cameroon (douala, yaounde). (3) Then: city expansion batch30 (next_p4_queue: Cordoba, Nuremberg, Hannover, Rouen, Charleroi, Namur, Wroclaw, Gdansk, Katowice, Aarhus -- needs fresh sourced research per city before build) + blog batch9 (is-lagos-safe-for-business-travel, close-protection-female-executives-middle-east, private-security-regulations-brazil, security-driver-nairobi-vs-taxi, executive-protection-bogota).
